sources.label: Data table – Carbon Design System
borrowed: Use a table-first queue for dense operational scanning.
Keep row actions compatible with toolbar and row interaction conventions.
rejected: Adding decorative dashboard visuals around the table.

sources.label: Text toolbar pattern – Carbon Design System
borrowed: Keep the action strip compact and bounded when horizontal space tightens.
rejected: A large toolbar that competes with the validation board.

sources.label: Overflow menu – Carbon Design System
borrowed: Use terse, direct action labels where density and space are constrained.
rejected: Turning the dominant workbench actions into an overflow-only affordance.

sources.label: How we redesigned the Linear UI
borrowed: Reduce visual noise while increasing hierarchy and density.
Let alignment and pane discipline carry the interface instead of extra chrome.
rejected: Loose card stacks with inconsistent internal alignment.

sources.label: A calmer interface for a product in motion
borrowed: Preserve high information density without making the workbench feel loud.
Keep headers and actions in predictable places.
rejected: Moving the action hierarchy between routes or panes.

sources.label: Nested Layouts | Vercel Academy
borrowed: Keep persistent chrome stable while child routes change.
Preserve section state across route changes inside the same shell.
rejected: Route transitions that remount the workbench chrome and lose continuity.

sources.label: Developing a Keyboard Interface | APG | WAI | W3C
borrowed: Keep focus order predictable across queue, workbench, support region, and dock.
Do not leave focus stranded on the document body after state changes.
rejected: Custom focus movement that hides the next actionable control.

sources.label: Layout Grid Examples | APG | WAI | W3C
borrowed: Use a single tab stop strategy only where it improves dense scanning, not everywhere.
Preserve directional scan cues for tightly packed interactive groups.
rejected: Treating the whole workbench as one composite grid.

sources.label: Toolbar Pattern | APG | WAI | W3C
borrowed: Reduce tab stops in compact action groups while keeping labels explicit.
rejected: Toolbar behavior for the main route toggles, which remain plain buttons.

sources.label: Dialog (Modal) Pattern | APG | WAI | W3C
borrowed: Return focus to the invoking control when a transient dialog closes.
Keep large semantic content out of unnecessary modal shells.
rejected: Detached modal compare or handoff workflows.

sources.label: Isolation | Playwright
borrowed: Use isolated browser contexts for each workbench proof path.
rejected: Cross-test state carry-over when checking continuity or session storage.

sources.label: Accessibility testing | Playwright
borrowed: Pair visual proof with focused accessibility assertions.
rejected: Treating one generic accessibility pass as the complete proof set.

sources.label: Snapshot testing | Playwright
borrowed: Use accessibility-tree snapshots as supporting evidence for workbench structure.
rejected: Relying on raw DOM snapshots instead of accessible structure.

sources.label: Visual comparisons | Playwright
borrowed: Capture deterministic desktop and narrow-screen baselines for queue and workbench states.
rejected: One combined screenshot that hides route-specific regressions.
